title: lilium philadelphicum subsp. umbellatum
intro: Lilium philadelphicum subsp. umbellatum, commonly known as the Wood Lily or Umbellate Wood Lily, is a perennial plant species in the lily family, native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects, which are attributed to its saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to aid in wound healing and reduce pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, tinctures, and salves, and can also be dried for later use in teas or as a spice.
